Scenario: Verify BIM 360 backup source can be configured
Steps:
1. Go to New Backup
2. Select BIM 360 as source
3. Enter credentials
4. Connect
ExpectedResult: BIM 360 connected — project list loads

Scenario: Verify BIM 360 project list shows all accessible projects
Steps:
1. Connect BIM 360 as backup source
2. Browse project list
ExpectedResult: All Admin and Member projects visible — archived excluded

Scenario: Verify BIM 360 backup scope can be narrowed to specific folders
Steps:
1. Connect BIM 360
2. Select specific projects/folders
ExpectedResult: Only selected scope backed up

Scenario: Verify Egnyte backup source can be configured
Steps:
1. Go to New Backup
2. Select Egnyte as source
3. Enter credentials
4. Connect
ExpectedResult: Egnyte connected — folder list loads

Scenario: Verify Egnyte folder tree browsable for backup scope selection
Steps:
1. Connect Egnyte
2. Browse folder tree
3. Select folders
ExpectedResult: Selected folders saved as backup scope

Scenario: Verify Amazon S3 can be set as backup target
Steps:
1. Select Amazon S3 as target
2. Enter Access Key / Secret / Bucket
3. Connect
ExpectedResult: S3 bucket accessible — backup target saved

Scenario: Verify S3 bucket path/prefix can be configured
Steps:
1. Set Amazon S3 as target
2. Enter bucket and prefix/folder path
ExpectedResult: Backup stored under correct prefix in S3

Scenario: Verify File System can be set as backup target
Steps:
1. Select File System as target
2. Enter UNC path or local path
3. Connect
ExpectedResult: File System path accessible — backup target saved

Scenario: Verify backup files appear in correct File System folder after backup
Steps:
1. Set File System target
2. Run backup
3. Check target folder
ExpectedResult: Backup files present in specified folder

Scenario: Verify S3 Compatible storage can be set as backup target
Steps:
1. Select S3 Compatible as target
2. Enter endpoint / key / secret / bucket
3. Connect
ExpectedResult: S3 Compatible connected — target saved

Scenario: Verify Azure Blob Storage can be set as backup target
Steps:
1. Select Azure Blob Storage as target
2. Enter connection string / container
3. Connect
ExpectedResult: Azure Blob connected — target saved

Scenario: Verify Azure Blob container path can be specified
Steps:
1. Set Azure Blob as target
2. Enter container and blob prefix
ExpectedResult: Backup stored under correct container path

Scenario: Verify backup can be scheduled with daily frequency
Steps:
1. Create backup plan
2. Set schedule = Daily
3. Set time
4. Save
ExpectedResult: Backup runs daily at specified time

Scenario: Verify backup can be scheduled with weekly frequency
Steps:
1. Create backup plan
2. Set schedule = Weekly
3. Select day and time
4. Save
ExpectedResult: Backup runs weekly on specified day/time

Scenario: Verify Next Backup Run Time required for weekly schedule on Standard Plan
Steps:
1. Standard Plan account
2. Create backup with weekly schedule
3. Skip run time selection
4. Save
ExpectedResult: Validation error — backup does not start without run time

Scenario: Verify Auto Backup can be enabled
Steps:
1. Open backup plan settings
2. Enable Auto Backup toggle
3. Save
ExpectedResult: Auto Backup enabled — runs on schedule automatically

Scenario: Verify manual backup can be started immediately
Steps:
1. Create backup plan
2. Click Run Now
ExpectedResult: Backup starts immediately — visible in Running Backups

Scenario: Verify backup status shows Running during execution
Steps:
1. Start backup
2. Observe status
ExpectedResult: Status = Running while backup is active

Scenario: Verify backup completes with status Completed
Steps:
1. Run full backup
2. Wait for completion
ExpectedResult: Status = Completed

Scenario: Verify incremental backup backs up only changed files
Steps:
1. Run full backup
2. Modify some source files
3. Run incremental backup
ExpectedResult: Only changed/new files included — not full re-backup

Scenario: Verify backup with failed files shows Completed with Errors
Steps: 1. Run backup with some inaccessible files
ExpectedResult: Status = Completed with Errors — failed file count shown

Scenario: Verify backup history lists all completed backups
Steps: 1. Navigate to Backup History for a plan
ExpectedResult: All past backup runs listed with date and status

Scenario: Verify backup history shows correct file counts per run
Steps:
1. Open backup history
2. Check file counts for each run
ExpectedResult: Counts match actual files backed up per run

Scenario: Verify backup history can be filtered by date range
Steps:
1. Set From–To date filter in history
2. Apply
ExpectedResult: Only runs within date range displayed

Scenario: Verify webhooks page loads with 100+ entries without timeout
Steps: 1. Open Admin > Webhooks when 100+ entries exist
ExpectedResult: Page loads successfully — no freeze or timeout

Scenario: Verify webhooks are processed with no stuck messages
Steps:
1. Trigger multiple webhook events
2. Monitor over time
ExpectedResult: All events processed — nothing stuck in Not Processed

Scenario: Verify multiple webhooks can be deleted in one action
Steps:
1. Select 2+ webhooks in Admin
2. Click Delete Selected
ExpectedResult: All selected webhooks deleted in one operation
